items.label: Accountability
items.looking_for: Names a privacy officer or contact and gives a way to reach them

items.label: Purpose identified
items.looking_for: States WHY data is being collected, in plain language, before/at collection

items.label: Consent is clear
items.looking_for: Explains how consent is obtained and how users can withdraw it

items.label: Data minimization
items.looking_for: Limits collection to what's needed for the stated purpose — no vague 'and other data'

items.label: Third-party sharing disclosed
items.looking_for: Lists categories of third parties data is shared with, and why

items.label: Data storage location disclosed
items.looking_for: Says WHERE data is stored (Canada / US / EU / 'globally') and notes cross-border transfer

items.label: Retention period stated
items.looking_for: Says how long data is kept, or the criteria used to decide

items.label: Security measures described
items.looking_for: Describes safeguards (encryption, access controls) in more than one generic sentence

items.label: User rights spelled out
items.looking_for: Tells users how to access, correct, or delete their data, and how to complain

items.label: Plain language
items.looking_for: Readable by a non-lawyer. No wall-of-text legalese. Has headings, structure

items.label: Last-updated date visible
items.looking_for: Shows when the policy was last revised

scoring_prompt:
You are reading a privacy policy and scoring it against a rubric grounded in Canada's PIPEDA fair information principles and Alberta's PIPA.

You are NOT making a legal determination. You are checking whether the policy TEXT discloses certain things clearly. Hedge accordingly. Never write "violates PIPEDA" — write "the policy does/does not disclose X clearly."

For each rubric item, return:
  - status: "yes" (clearly addressed), "partial" (mentioned vaguely), or "no" (missing/unclear)
  - evidence: 1-2 short quotes or paraphrases from the policy (under 15 words each)
  - note: one short sentence explaining your call

Rubric:
%RUBRIC_JSON%

Privacy policy text (may be truncated):
---
%POLICY_TEXT%
---

Return ONLY valid JSON, no preamble, no markdown fences, in this exact shape:
{
  "items": {
    "<key>": {"status": "yes|partial|no", "evidence": "...", "note": "..."},
    ...
  },
  "summary": "2-3 sentence plain-English summary of the policy's strengths and weaknesses"
}
